actioned: sev-3: shadow-init test helpers (computeShowMakeAdmin/computeIsRowAdmin re-implemented in the test) duplicated the production gating logic — FIXED: exported computeShowMakeAdmin() + isRowAdmin() from MemberList.tsx and bound the test to those real predicates so gating-logic drift now fails the test
watch: sev-2: removalPendingBadge copy is defined+tested but rendered by no component (S4 uses leavePendingBadge for all pending-removal rows). Carried to S6 as the leave-vs-removal cause-distinction decision.

review.stage2_codex: deferred-to-precommit-gate: S4 is pure-presentational, introduces no new cross-module seams (only optional props consumed in S6), and the resumable Opus reviewer (whole-assembly model) was clean. Cross-vendor Codex runs full-tree over the whole epic at the Step 6 pre-commit review gate.

implementation_summary: Added four optional props to MemberListProps and MemberListItemProps: adminPubkeys, isCurrentUserAdmin, onMakeAdmin, pendingRemovalPubkeys. Per-row derivations compute isRowAdmin (case-insensitive), showMakeAdmin (gated on AC-GRANT-1/3/4 conditions), and isPendingRemoval. MemberListItem gains three new visual elements: an Admin badge (purple, data-testid admin-badge-*), a pending-removal badge (orange, data-testid removal-pending-*), and a Make-admin trigger button + confirmation modal (mirror of the cancel-invite modal; useDisclosure + isMakingAdmin state; finally-block close; data-testid make-admin-* and make-admin-confirm-*). All copy is resolved in MemberList.map() and passed as flat string props to MemberListItem. Zero useMarmot() or useContext() calls introduced.

test_counts.note: After review remediation the test binds to exported predicates; still 24 tests, all passing.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: The vitest config only includes *.test.ts (not *.tsx) and @testing-library/react is not installed, so DOM-level component tests are impossible in the unit suite. The spec asked for a .tsx test with rendered assertions; it was written as a .test.ts with logic-level helpers instead, and DOM assertions defer to the e2e suite once S6 wires the component. The Opus review then (correctly) flagged those helpers as a shadow-init duplicate of the production gating logic — remediated by exporting computeShowMakeAdmin/isRowAdmin and binding the test to them. The combined lesson: when a DOM renderer is unavailable, the right move is to extract the logic as an exported pure helper from the start (so the test binds to production code), rather than re-implement the predicate in the test.
retrospective.surprised_by: VQ-S4-PI-003: the spec mentions both 'leavePendingBadge' and 'removalPendingBadge' for the pending-removal row, but only one pendingRemovalPubkeys prop is defined with no signal to distinguish the two badge variants by cause. The implementation uses leavePendingBadge consistently; the cause-distinction is carried to S6 as a watch item.
